name: Armedilla Trail
name_original: Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla — circular por el Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65)
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65) to explore the historic Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla ruins.
region_intro: Hiking around Cogeces Del Monte offers diverse landscapes situated between the Campo de Peñafiel and Tierra de Pinares de Cuéllar regions. The terrain features a network of moors, small valleys, and extensive pine forests. Hikers can explore trails that traverse these woodlands, alongside meadows, streams, and fresh fountains. The area also includes elevated calcareous páramos and mixed forests, providing varied natural environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Cogeces Del Monte The most popular hiking route is Atalaya Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Duero valley and the Atalaya viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is Duero Trail (GR 14), a moderate 11.4 miles (18.4 km) path. This trail follows the Duero river, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Sendero del Valcorba (PRC-VA 21) — circular Santibáñez de Valcorba, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Cogeces Del Monte is defined by pine forests, moors, and small valleys, with routes offering options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Cogeces Del Monte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Cogeces Del Monte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: Cogeces del Monte is characterized by a diverse landscape, including a labyrinth of moors and small valleys, extensive pine forests, meadows, poplar groves, and streams. You'll also find elevated calcareous páramos and mixed forests of pine, holm oak, gall oak, and junipers, offering varied natural environments and fantastic views.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3-mile (5.3 km) route that provides pleasant views of the Duero valley and the Atalaya viewpoint. Overall, there are 10 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can visit near the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can explore several interesting sites. Just 4 kilometers from town are the impressive ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3589685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla&lt;/a&gt;, a Cultural Interest Asset. Another unique attraction is the Parque Etnográfico de la Arquitectura Pastoril, which reconstructs shepherd life. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084838369/armedilla-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armedilla Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can lead you towards the monastery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077535602/sendero-del-valcorba-prc-va-21-circular-santibanez-de-valcorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Valcorba (PRC®-VA 21) — circular Santibáñez de Valcorba&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.2-mile (8.3 km) loop through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: The region's natural features, including numerous fresh fountains, make it enjoyable even during the summer. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant foliage or blooming wildflowers. Winter hiking is also possible, especially through the extensive pine forests.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: Many trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, especially those that traverse pine forests and open landscapes. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific regulations may vary, so check local signage.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The extensive pine forests and diverse landscapes, including moors and valleys, provide habitats for various wildlife. Hikers often report hearing and seeing numerous bird species. The quiet walks through the forests are ideal for observing nature, though larger mammals are generally shy.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route's length and your pace. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084789912/duero-trail-gr-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail (GR 14)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.4 miles (18.4 km), might take around 4 hours 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer water features?
A: Yes, the region features streams and numerous fresh fountains, even in summer. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084789912/duero-trail-gr-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail (GR 14)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, follows the Duero river, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape and opportunities to enjoy riverside scenery.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cogeces Del Monte?
A: The hiking routes in Cogeces Del Monte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet woodlands, varied terrain, and the opportunity to enjoy nature away from crowds. Over 30 hikers have used komoot to explore the area.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes?
A: While there are no routes classified as 'difficult' in the immediate area, there are 13 moderate trails that offer longer distances and more varied terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084789912/duero-trail-gr-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail (GR 14)&lt;/a&gt;, at 11.4 miles (18.4 km), is a good option for those seeking a longer, moderate challenge.
tips:
Catholic sanctuary of Cogeces del Monte dating from the 16th century with late Gothic style.
Inside there is a Romanesque image of the Virgen de la Armedilla, from the monastery of Santa María de la Armedilla (a few kilometers away).

Ruins of what was a monastery of the Order of San Jerónimo dating from the 15th century. Although you can only go around it from the outside, it is beautiful to visit since it gives rise to the imagination of what this sanctuary would have been.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla — circular por el Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65)?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 2 hours and 44 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend exploring the sights along the way.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered to have a low to moderate difficulty. It's classified as a "Pequeño Recorrido" (PR) route, meaning it's designed to be completed in a single day and is accessible for most hikers, including beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain as it follows the valley formed by the Cogeces stream. You'll encounter flatter sections along the valley floor, as well as gentle inclines and declines associated with the modest elevation changes. The route is known for its historical-artistic character, connecting the town of Cogeces del Monte with the monastery ruins, and may feature pine forests, fields of dryland crops, and riverside vegetation typical of the Valladolid province.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Armedilla Monastery, dating back to the 15th century. Although largely in ruins, its exterior perimeter is safely visitable, offering a profound sense of history. The trail also connects to the town of Cogeces del Monte, where you can find the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Cogeces del Monte.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that connects Cogeces del Monte with the Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla. While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, it's common for PR routes in Spain to have designated parking areas near the starting point in or around the associated towns. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival in Cogeces del Monte.

faqs.question: Is the Monasterio de Nuestra Señora de la Armedilla — circular por el Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas of Spain are dog-friendly, especially if they are not within a strictly protected natural park. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers. Please be mindful of any specific signage regarding dogs at the monastery ruins or along the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially less favorable conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: As a "Pequeño Recorrido" (PR) trail, it is highly unlikely that you will need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65) itself. Access to the exterior perimeter of the Armedilla Monastery ruins is also typically free.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail, like cafes or restrooms?
faqs.answer: The trail connects to the town of Cogeces del Monte. You can expect to find basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly restrooms within the town before or after your hike. There are no facilities directly on the trail itself, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65)?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, and snacks. Depending on the season, a hat, sunscreen, and insect repellent can be useful. A light jacket might be good for cooler mornings or evenings, especially in spring and autumn. Don't forget your camera to capture the historical ruins and natural beauty!

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is officially known as the Sendero de la Armedilla (PRC-VA 65), which covers 100% of this specific trail. Additionally, small sections of the route overlap with paths named Plaza de la Constitución, accounting for about 4% of the total distance.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Cogeces del Monte
